item.title: Business Card Maker Software [Office Apps]
item.itunes:title: Business Card Maker Software [Office Apps]
item.itunes:author: barcodesoftware
item.itunes:summary:
Business Card Maker Software
(Office Apps)
Users can successfully downloaded Business Card Maker Software from website www.barcodelabelmaker.net which helps to generate and develop different business cards consists of various types and patterns in simplest and user friendly manner. Using advanced designing techniques and tools which consist of pencil, text, barcode value, circle, ellipse, you can develop multiple patterns of schools identity cards in easiest manner.
[read more]

item.title: Download Camera Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:title: Download Camera Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:author: memorycardrecovery
item.itunes:summary:
Download Camera Recovery Software
(Utilities)
How to recover lost file folders from any formatted digital camera memory card? Comprehensive Download Recovery Software is available at www.digitalcamerarecovery.org facilitates to salvage accidentally erased lost photographs due to software/hardware malfunction, virus attacks, formatted digicam memory card, human error, improper usages of device, power failure and more other reasons occurred. Do you want to retrieve deleted birthday party photos from professional digital camera? Highly advance Download Recovery Software from www.digitalcamerarecovery.org provides option to salvage missing and misplaced wedding anniversaries audio and video clips files from compact flash digital camera in few seconds.

[read more]

item.title: Drive Recovery Software Professional [Utilities]
item.itunes:title: Drive Recovery Software Professional [Utilities]
item.itunes:author: recoverysoftware
item.itunes:summary:
Drive Recovery Software Professional
(Utilities)
Click on www.driverecoverysoftware.org for downloading advanced Drive Recovery Software Professional that supports instant recovery of pictures, folders, documents and other essential files from formatted mass storage media. Best featured DDR recovery file salvage software empowers advanced disk scanning mechanism to search and restore lost data as per user requirements. User friendly Drive Recovery Software Professional offers rich quality GUI to salvage lost data without doing additional effort.

item.title: Windows Fat Drive Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:title: Windows Fat Drive Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:author: recoverysoftware
item.itunes:summary:
Windows Fat Drive Recovery Software
(Utilities)
Visit website www.driverecoverysoftware.org and quickly download restore program to recover deleted/formatted mp3/mp4 music file and folder without any error from Windows based Fat file system partitioned. Windows Fat Drive Recovery Software is used to retrieve lost crucial document, digital images due to electricity failure from HDD drive and also compatible with all types of hard disk like ATA, EIDE, SCSI, SATA, IDE etc.
[read more]

item.title: GODSPEED CONTROLLER AIM [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:title: GODSPEED CONTROLLER AIM [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:author: revwhiteshadow
item.itunes:summary:
GODSPEED CONTROLLER AIM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
Godspeed Controller AIM is a revolutionary Magisk module developed by revWhiteShadow, designed to enhance the gaming experience on joystick-supported Android games. This innovative module tackles the common issue of recoil in shooting games by significantly reducing it when using a joystick for aiming. By seamlessly integrating with the Magisk framework, this module offers an easy-to-install solution for gamers seeking improved precision and control.
With Godspeed Controller AIM, players can enjoy a more immersive and accurate gaming experience. The module intelligently analyzes joystick movements and applies advanced algorithms to mitigate recoil, allowing gamers to maintain better control over their aiming. This reduction in recoil ensures smoother and more precise shots, leading to improved accuracy and potentially higher success rates in combat scenarios.

Whether it's an intense battle royale or a tactical first-person shooter, the Godspeed Controller AIM Magisk module provides a competitive edge by eliminating the frustration caused by excessive recoil. By optimizing joystick functionality and minimizing the impact of recoil, revWhiteShadow's module empowers gamers to focus on their strategies and gameplay, ultimately elevating their overall performance.

With its user-friendly installation process and compatibility with various joystick-supported Android games, Godspeed Controller AIM offers a convenient solution for gamers looking to enhance their gaming experience. By harnessing the power of this module, players can take their skills to the next level and dominate the virtual battlefield with superior aim control.
[read more]
item.description:
GODSPEED CONTROLLER AIM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
Godspeed Controller AIM is a revolutionary Magisk module developed by revWhiteShadow, designed to enhance the gaming experience on joystick-supported Android games. This innovative module tackles the common issue of recoil in shooting games by significantly reducing it when using a joystick for aiming. By seamlessly integrating with the Magisk framework, this module offers an easy-to-install solution for gamers seeking improved precision and control.
With Godspeed Controller AIM, players can enjoy a more immersive and accurate gaming experience. The module intelligently analyzes joystick movements and applies advanced algorithms to mitigate recoil, allowing gamers to maintain better control over their aiming. This reduction in recoil ensures smoother and more precise shots, leading to improved accuracy and potentially higher success rates in combat scenarios.

Whether it's an intense battle royale or a tactical first-person shooter, the Godspeed Controller AIM Magisk module provides a competitive edge by eliminating the frustration caused by excessive recoil. By optimizing joystick functionality and minimizing the impact of recoil, revWhiteShadow's module empowers gamers to focus on their strategies and gameplay, ultimately elevating their overall performance.

With its user-friendly installation process and compatibility with various joystick-supported Android games, Godspeed Controller AIM offers a convenient solution for gamers looking to enhance their gaming experience. By harnessing the power of this module, players can take their skills to the next level and dominate the virtual battlefield with superior aim control.
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
GODSPEED CONTROLLER AIM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
Godspeed Controller AIM is a revolutionary Magisk module developed by revWhiteShadow, designed to enhance the gaming experience on joystick-supported Android games. This innovative module tackles the common issue of recoil in shooting games by significantly reducing it when using a joystick for aiming. By seamlessly integrating with the Magisk framework, this module offers an easy-to-install solution for gamers seeking improved precision and control.
With Godspeed Controller AIM, players can enjoy a more immersive and accurate gaming experience. The module intelligently analyzes joystick movements and applies advanced algorithms to mitigate recoil, allowing gamers to maintain better control over their aiming. This reduction in recoil ensures smoother and more precise shots, leading to improved accuracy and potentially higher success rates in combat scenarios.

Whether it's an intense battle royale or a tactical first-person shooter, the Godspeed Controller AIM Magisk module provides a competitive edge by eliminating the frustration caused by excessive recoil. By optimizing joystick functionality and minimizing the impact of recoil, revWhiteShadow's module empowers gamers to focus on their strategies and gameplay, ultimately elevating their overall performance.

With its user-friendly installation process and compatibility with various joystick-supported Android games, Godspeed Controller AIM offers a convenient solution for gamers looking to enhance their gaming experience. By harnessing the power of this module, players can take their skills to the next level and dominate the virtual battlefield with superior aim control.
[read more]

item.title: Packaging Label Barcode Generator [Office Apps]
item.itunes:title: Packaging Label Barcode Generator [Office Apps]
item.itunes:author: barcodesoftware
item.itunes:summary:
Packaging Label Barcode Generator
(Office Apps)
Want to create unique barcode holograms for your packaging supply and distribution industry? Visit company www.barcodelabelmaker.net website to download Packaging Label Barcode Generator software which makes distinct barcode holograms using linear and 2D font symbologies including Aztec, UPCA, UPCE, DataMatrix, Maxicode, industrial 2 of 5, LOGMARS, Coda bar etc. Professional barcode designer software application makes stickers for packaging supply and distribution industry and facilitates saving barcode images in jpeg, bmp, exif, tiff file extensions.

[read more]

item.title: STRP x ZRAM [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:title: STRP x ZRAM [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:author: crankv2
item.itunes:summary:
STRP x ZRAM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
[color=#FF5733][size=5]STRP x ZRAM[/size][/color]
[color=#FF5733]STRP x ZRAM is a module that provides additional ZRAM (compressed RAM) partition options ranging from 1 GB up to 15 GB on your Android device.[/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Requirements[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]- Magisk 23.0+
- Latest Busybox or Brutal
- Android 7.0+[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## About ZRAM and Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### ZRAM[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]ZRAM compresses unused apps within system RAM, allowing the system to swap less needed processes to the ZRAM partition for faster access at a later time, instead of terminating them. The value you set determines the percentage of RAM allocated to the ZRAM partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Swap uses a portion of the SD card as virtual RAM. The phone attempts to keep as much data in RAM as possible until it's full, then starts using the swap partition on the SD card. It moves inactive memory blocks to the SD card to free up RAM for active processes.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## How to Determine ZRAM or Swap Availability[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]There are two methods:[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### First Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Open a terminal emulator (e.g., Termux) and type:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/swaps
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This command checks if your device has any Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### Second Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]If your phone/ROM features "RAM PLUS," you can change ZRAM between 1 and 15 GB.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How much storage will it consume?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]The storage consumption depends on the option you choose during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Is it safe to use?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Yes, it's safe.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How to remove or revert to the stock configuration?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Simply remove the module using Magisk.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm experiencing lag after rebooting.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This is normal; give your phone some time to adapt to the new Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm facing random lags.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Try using a lower ZRAM size during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How can I verify if it's working?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]You can use any partition-checking application, like DiskSpace, or run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/meminfo
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Look for 'SwapTotal' and 'SwapFree' to see if the allocated swap space has changed.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Opening the ZRAM Configuration Menu[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]To open the ZRAM Configuration Menu:[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]1. Make sure you have a rooted Android device with SuperSU or a similar root management tool installed.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]2. Open a terminal emulator or ADB shell on your device.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]3. Run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c strp-zram
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]4. Grant superuser (root) access when prompted.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]5. The ZRAM Configuration Menu will appear, allowing you to configure your ZRAM size or disable ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]6. Follow the on-screen instructions to make your selection and configure ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]7. After making your choice, the script will inform you of the successful configuration and may prompt you to reboot your device for changes to take effect.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]8. You can exit the menu by selecting "Exit."[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This menu is intended for advanced users with root access to their Android device and a basic understanding of system configurations.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## License[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0. For details, please see the License at ...
https://github.com/CRANKV2/ZRAM/blob/main/LICENSE[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Join the Community[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]For discussions and support, join the STRP x UNIVERSE at
https://t.me/AndroidRootModulesCommunity[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=5]STAY FAST ⚡️[/size][/color]
[read more]
item.description:
STRP x ZRAM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
[color=#FF5733][size=5]STRP x ZRAM[/size][/color]
[color=#FF5733]STRP x ZRAM is a module that provides additional ZRAM (compressed RAM) partition options ranging from 1 GB up to 15 GB on your Android device.[/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Requirements[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]- Magisk 23.0+
- Latest Busybox or Brutal
- Android 7.0+[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## About ZRAM and Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### ZRAM[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]ZRAM compresses unused apps within system RAM, allowing the system to swap less needed processes to the ZRAM partition for faster access at a later time, instead of terminating them. The value you set determines the percentage of RAM allocated to the ZRAM partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Swap uses a portion of the SD card as virtual RAM. The phone attempts to keep as much data in RAM as possible until it's full, then starts using the swap partition on the SD card. It moves inactive memory blocks to the SD card to free up RAM for active processes.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## How to Determine ZRAM or Swap Availability[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]There are two methods:[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### First Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Open a terminal emulator (e.g., Termux) and type:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/swaps
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This command checks if your device has any Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### Second Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]If your phone/ROM features "RAM PLUS," you can change ZRAM between 1 and 15 GB.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How much storage will it consume?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]The storage consumption depends on the option you choose during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Is it safe to use?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Yes, it's safe.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How to remove or revert to the stock configuration?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Simply remove the module using Magisk.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm experiencing lag after rebooting.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This is normal; give your phone some time to adapt to the new Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm facing random lags.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Try using a lower ZRAM size during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How can I verify if it's working?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]You can use any partition-checking application, like DiskSpace, or run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/meminfo
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Look for 'SwapTotal' and 'SwapFree' to see if the allocated swap space has changed.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Opening the ZRAM Configuration Menu[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]To open the ZRAM Configuration Menu:[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]1. Make sure you have a rooted Android device with SuperSU or a similar root management tool installed.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]2. Open a terminal emulator or ADB shell on your device.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]3. Run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c strp-zram
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]4. Grant superuser (root) access when prompted.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]5. The ZRAM Configuration Menu will appear, allowing you to configure your ZRAM size or disable ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]6. Follow the on-screen instructions to make your selection and configure ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]7. After making your choice, the script will inform you of the successful configuration and may prompt you to reboot your device for changes to take effect.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]8. You can exit the menu by selecting "Exit."[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This menu is intended for advanced users with root access to their Android device and a basic understanding of system configurations.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## License[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0. For details, please see the License at ...
https://github.com/CRANKV2/ZRAM/blob/main/LICENSE[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Join the Community[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]For discussions and support, join the STRP x UNIVERSE at
https://t.me/AndroidRootModulesCommunity[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=5]STAY FAST ⚡️[/size][/color]
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
STRP x ZRAM
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
[color=#FF5733][size=5]STRP x ZRAM[/size][/color]
[color=#FF5733]STRP x ZRAM is a module that provides additional ZRAM (compressed RAM) partition options ranging from 1 GB up to 15 GB on your Android device.[/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Requirements[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]- Magisk 23.0+
- Latest Busybox or Brutal
- Android 7.0+[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## About ZRAM and Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### ZRAM[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]ZRAM compresses unused apps within system RAM, allowing the system to swap less needed processes to the ZRAM partition for faster access at a later time, instead of terminating them. The value you set determines the percentage of RAM allocated to the ZRAM partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Swap[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Swap uses a portion of the SD card as virtual RAM. The phone attempts to keep as much data in RAM as possible until it's full, then starts using the swap partition on the SD card. It moves inactive memory blocks to the SD card to free up RAM for active processes.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## How to Determine ZRAM or Swap Availability[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]There are two methods:[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### First Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Open a terminal emulator (e.g., Termux) and type:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/swaps
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This command checks if your device has any Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]### Second Method[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]If your phone/ROM features "RAM PLUS," you can change ZRAM between 1 and 15 GB.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How much storage will it consume?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]The storage consumption depends on the option you choose during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### Is it safe to use?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Yes, it's safe.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How to remove or revert to the stock configuration?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Simply remove the module using Magisk.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm experiencing lag after rebooting.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This is normal; give your phone some time to adapt to the new Swap Partition.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### I'm facing random lags.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Try using a lower ZRAM size during installation.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]### How can I verify if it's working?[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]You can use any partition-checking application, like DiskSpace, or run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c cat /proc/meminfo
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]Look for 'SwapTotal' and 'SwapFree' to see if the allocated swap space has changed.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Opening the ZRAM Configuration Menu[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]To open the ZRAM Configuration Menu:[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]1. Make sure you have a rooted Android device with SuperSU or a similar root management tool installed.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]2. Open a terminal emulator or ADB shell on your device.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]3. Run the following command:[/size][/color]

```
su -c strp-zram
```

[color=#FF5733][size=3]4. Grant superuser (root) access when prompted.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]5. The ZRAM Configuration Menu will appear, allowing you to configure your ZRAM size or disable ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]6. Follow the on-screen instructions to make your selection and configure ZRAM.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]7. After making your choice, the script will inform you of the successful configuration and may prompt you to reboot your device for changes to take effect.[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]8. You can exit the menu by selecting "Exit."[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This menu is intended for advanced users with root access to their Android device and a basic understanding of system configurations.[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## License[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0. For details, please see the License at ...
https://github.com/CRANKV2/ZRAM/blob/main/LICENSE[/size][/color]

[color=#607D8B][size=3]## Join the Community[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=3]For discussions and support, join the STRP x UNIVERSE at
https://t.me/AndroidRootModulesCommunity[/size][/color]

[color=#FF5733][size=5]STAY FAST ⚡️[/size][/color]
[read more]

item.title: Retail Barcodes Designing Software [Office Apps]
item.itunes:title: Retail Barcodes Designing Software [Office Apps]
item.itunes:author: businessbarcode
item.itunes:summary:
Retail Barcodes Designing Software
(Office Apps)
Retail Barcodes Software empowers to design professional retail business and inventory control barcodes tags with latest linear and 2D font standard. Barcode Creator Application instantly produces multiple copies of barcode sticker in various shapes like line, text, pencil, rectangle ellipse, arc and star object.

[read more]

item.title: Pen Drive Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:title: Pen Drive Recovery Software [Utilities]
item.itunes:author: memorycardrecovery
item.itunes:summary:
Pen Drive Recovery Software
(Utilities)
www.digitalcamerarecovery.org enables to download Pen Drive Recovery Software at affordable cost is able to regain logically crashed or formatted video clips and other files from pen drive in less effort without requiring any expert knowledge. Pen drive data restoration application provides ability to restore office files deleted from storage media due to several types of data loss condition like human fault, virus attack, accidentally corrupted device etc.
[read more]
